lede: A look at June 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Fran González (footballer, born 2005) and Jarret Stoll, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Wagner Group
events.desc: The Wagner Group led by Yevgeny Prigozhin launches an insurrection against the Russian government.

events.title: Julia Gillard
events.desc: Julia Gillard assumes office as the first female Prime Minister of Australia.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Collar, the first British Commando raid on occupied France, by No 11 Independent Company.

events.title: Siamese revolution of 1932
events.desc: A bloodless revolution instigated by the People's Party ends the absolute power of King Prajadhipok of Siam (now Thailand).

events.title: O Canada
events.desc: First performance of O Canada at the Congrès national des Canadiens-Français. The song would later become the national anthem of Canada.

events.title: Napoleonic Wars
events.desc: Napoleonic Wars: Napoleon's Grande Armée crosses the Neman river beginning the invasion of Russia.

events.title: Nativity of John the Baptist
events.desc: On the Feast of St. John the Baptist, Bach leads the first performance of his Christ unser Herr zum Jordan kam, BWV 7, the third cantata of his chorale cantata cycle.

events.title: Dancing mania
events.desc: A sudden outbreak of St. John's Dance causes people in the streets of Aachen, Germany, to experience hallucinations and begin to jump and twitch uncontrollably until they collapse from exhaustion.

events.title: Hundred Years' War
events.desc: Hundred Years' War: Battle of Sluys: The French fleet is almost completely destroyed by the English fleet commanded personally by King Edward III.

events.title: First War of Scottish Independence
events.desc: First War of Scottish Independence: The Battle of Bannockburn concludes with a decisive victory by Scottish forces led by Robert the Bruce.

deaths.text: Roméo LeBlanc, Canadian journalist and politician, 25th Governor General of Canada
Jean Vallerand, Canadian violinist, composer, and conductor
Emil Seidel, American politician, Mayor of Milwaukee
Pieter Burman the Younger, Dutch philologist and academic

observances.text: Army Day or Battle of Carabobo Day (Venezuela)
Christian feast day: María Guadalupe García Zavala
Christian feast day: Nativity of Saint John the Baptist
Inti Raymi, a winter solstice festival and a New Year in the Andes of the Southern Hemisphere (Sacsayhuamán)

St John's Day and the second day of the Midsummer celebrations (although this is not the astronomical summer solstice, see June 20) (Roman Catholic Church, Europe), and its related observances: Enyovden (Bulgaria)
